visa_types.name: Schengen Visas
visa_types.documents_required: The checklists of required visa application documents are available in the online visa application portal of the Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland (https://finlandvisa.fi/checklist)
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:Schengen Visas]

Methods of payment for visa fees:
- Card payment at the Finland Visa Application Centre on the day of appointment
- Cash payment at the Finland Visa Application Centre on the day of appointment

Service charge applicable when applying at the Finland Visa Application Centre:

[Table:Schengen Visas]

*Service fee is inclusive of tax. 
Click for the price list of additional services. (http://liftpoc.vfsglobal.com.s3-website.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/blr/en/fin/premium-services)

Please Note:
- Visa and Service fees and all additional fees are paid in Belorussian Rubles (BYN) at the rate agreed additionally on the day of payment.
- The service fee is collected per each application and in addition to the visa fee.
- Service fee includes courier cost of decision to applicant’s address.
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Application Centre should pay all the fees at the cash counter in the center.
visa_types.processing_time:
We recommend you to apply  at least three (3) weeks before the planned trip. Some applications may require  further processing. Please note that all applications are processed  individually. The Embassy of Finland in Moscow may request further  documentation, information or you may be called for an interview which can  prolong the processing time. 
               Note also that courier of application and decision envelope from Belarus to  Moscow and back takes several days.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
All Belarus citizens when  entering Finland and other countries under the Schengen agreement have to apply  for a visa. Before applying, please ensure you are very clear on your ‘purpose  of visit’. Estonia is representing Finland in Belarus and therefore Schengen  visa to Finland has to be obtained from the Embassy of Estonia.
 All seasonal visa  applicants are allowed to submit application in Visa Application Centre in  Minsk.
Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the start of the planned journey. The application process begins when the applicant personally submits his or her visa application and other supporting documents to the VAC or the mission.
 Click on the  other tabs on this page to view more information about the visa application  process. 
Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/fin/book-an-appointment) to book an appointment
Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/fin/) to go back to the homepage.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
All Belarus citizens when  entering Finland and other countries under the Schengen agreement have to apply  for a visa. Before applying, please ensure you are very clear on your ‘purpose  of visit’. Estonia is representing Finland in Belarus and therefore Schengen  visa to Finland has to be obtained from the Embassy of Estonia.
 All seasonal visa  applicants are allowed to submit application in Visa Application Centre in  Minsk.
Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the start of the planned journey. The application process begins when the applicant personally submits his or her visa application and other supporting documents to the VAC or the mission.
 Click on the  other tabs on this page to view more information about the visa application  process. 
Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/fin/book-an-appointment) to book an appointment
Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/blr/en/fin/) to go back to the homepage.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Schengen Visas]

Methods of payment for visa fees:
- Card payment at the Finland Visa Application Centre on the day of appointment
- Cash payment at the Finland Visa Application Centre on the day of appointment

Service charge applicable when applying at the Finland Visa Application Centre:

[Table:Schengen Visas]

*Service fee is inclusive of tax. 
Click for the price list of additional services. (http://liftpoc.vfsglobal.com.s3-website.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/blr/en/fin/premium-services)

Please Note:
- Visa and Service fees and all additional fees are paid in Belorussian Rubles (BYN) at the rate agreed additionally on the day of payment.
- The service fee is collected per each application and in addition to the visa fee.
- Service fee includes courier cost of decision to applicant’s address.
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.
- The applicants who submit their applications at the Application Centre should pay all the fees at the cash counter in the center.

## Documents Required
The checklists of required visa application documents are available in the online visa application portal of the Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland (https://finlandvisa.fi/checklist)

## Photo Specifications
To see the  visa application photo specifications, please, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/798ogZEPm06rSbOtRXCy6k/a14f9f00397b1475a8c7d4692cb9d804/photograph-guidelines.pdf)

## Travel Medical Insurance
Each client (including  children) must have an insurance policy. An client for a multiple-entry visa  may provide an insurance policy, which is valid during the first trip. The  client undertakes to purchase an insurance policy for further trips by signing  the visa application.
               Basic requirements for an  insurance policy:
- Insurance validity area should       cover all the Schengen countries, no areal restrictions (for example       “Finland, Schengen”) are allowed.
- Without franchise.
- The insurance coverage limit       should be not less than 30 000 euros or equivalent amount in other       currency (for example, 40 000 dollars).
- Policies should insure the       costs in case of sudden illness or accident and assistance on site,       including the costs of medical repatriation to the place of permanent       residence of the insured person or the repatriation in case of death.
Insurance policies filled  in by hand are not accepted.
 A client always needs to  present an original insurance policy and a copy if needed.

## Processing Time
We recommend you to apply  at least three (3) weeks before the planned trip. Some applications may require  further processing. Please note that all applications are processed  individually. The Embassy of Finland in Moscow may request further  documentation, information or you may be called for an interview which can  prolong the processing time. 
               Note also that courier of application and decision envelope from Belarus to  Moscow and back takes several days.
